categories.description: All configuration items related to the core gameplay
categories.name: Gameplay Settings

options.description: minecolonies.config.enablecitizenvoices.comment
options.name: minecolonies.config.enablecitizenvoices

options.description: By default, when placing a schematic near other buildings, the already-placed buildings will show as if they were at level 5 and have a blue outline around them. You can disable that by setting this to false.
options.name: Neighbor Building Rendering

options.description: How close a building needs to be to another to be considered a neighbor, in blocks. -1 = intersecting, 0 = touching
options.name: Neighbor Building Range

options.description: How close (in blocks) you need to be to see work orders while wearing the Build Goggles.
options.name: Build Goggles Range

options.description: When true, the colony borders shown when holding the build tool will be in the colony's team color. When false, the colony you're inside will be white and any other colony will be red.
options.name: Show Colony Borders in Team Colors

options.description: Should special holiday content be displayed?
options.name: Holiday Features

categories.description: All configurations related to the request system
categories.name: Request System Settings

options.description: Should the request system show debug information in the debug.log? Useful if malfunctioning.
options.name: Enable Debug Logging

categories.description: All configuration items related to the core gameplay
categories.name: Gameplay Settings

options.description: Whether to generate supply ships and camps in loot chests.
options.name: Supply Loot

options.description: Verbosity of pathfinding debug messages.
options.name: Pathfinding Debug Verbosity

options.description: Minimum number of consecutive rails for citizens to use them.
options.name: Minimum Rails

options.description: Amount of additional threads to be used for pathfinding.
options.name: Pathfinding Max Thread Count

categories.description: All configurations related to the request system
categories.name: Request System Settings

options.description: Should the request system creatively resolve (if possible) when the player is required to resolve a request? This is a debugging tool and can take a very long time to resolve a request.
options.name: Creatively Resolve Requests

options.description: Should colony protection be enabled?
options.name: Enable Colony Protection

options.description: Independent from the colony protection, should explosions be turned off inside colonies? DAMAGE_NOTHING prevents explosions completely. DAMAGE_PLAYERS, allows explosions to damage players and hostile mobs, but not blocks or neutral or friendly mobs. DAMAGE_ENTITIES allows damage to all entities. DAMAGE_EVERYTHING allows explosions to damage entities and blocks.
options.name: Turn off Explosions in Colonies

categories.description: All configuration related to colony claims
categories.name: Claim Settings

options.description: Maximum claim range for a colony. This is the radius, measured in chunks.
options.name: Maximum Claim Range for Colonies

options.description: The minimum distance (in chunks) between colonies.
options.name: Minimum Colony Distance

options.description: Initial claim size for a colony. This is the radius, measured in chunks.
options.name: Initial Colony Claim Size

options.description: Max distance (in blocks) from world spawn for a colony.
options.name: Max Distance from World Spawn

options.description: Min distance (in blocks) from world spawn for a colony.
options.name: Min Distance from World Spawn

categories.description: All configuration items related to the combat elements of MineColonies
categories.name: Combat Settings

options.description: minecolonies.config.dobarbariansspawn.comment
options.name: minecolonies.config.dobarbariansspawn

options.name: minecolonies.config.barbarianhordedifficulty

options.description: minecolonies.config.maxbarbariansize.comment
options.name: minecolonies.config.maxbarbariansize

options.description: minecolonies.config.dobarbariansbreakthroughwalls.comment
options.name: minecolonies.config.dobarbariansbreakthroughwalls

options.description: The average number of nights between raids.
options.name: Average Number of Nights Between Raids

options.description: The minimum number of nights between raids.
options.name: Minimum Number of Nights Between Raids

options.description: Should mobs attack citizens?
options.name: Mobs Attack Citizens

options.description: minecolonies.config.shouldraiderbreakdoors.comment
options.name: minecolonies.config.shouldraiderbreakdoors

options.description: minecolonies.config.guarddamagemultiplier.comment
options.name: minecolonies.config.guarddamagemultiplier

options.description: Health multiplier for all Guards.
options.name: Guard Health Multiplier

options.description: Turn on MineColonies PVP mode (colonies can be destroyed and griefed under certain conditions).
options.name: PVP Mode

categories.description: All configuration related to mod compatibility
categories.name: Mod Compatibility Settings

options.description: When loading recipes, generate audit CSV files to help debug datapacks or extra mods.
options.name: Audit Crafting Tags

options.description: Enable inventory debugging.
options.name: Debug Inventories

options.description: Turn this on if you're using this world for blueprint building and scanning.
options.name: Blueprint Build Mode

categories.description: All configurations related to the MineColonies commands
categories.name: Command Settings

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc rtp command?
options.name: Can Players Use the RTP Command

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc colony teleport command?
options.name: Can Players Use the Colony TP Command

options.description: Can players teleport to allied colonies?
options.name: Can Players TP to Allies

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc home command? Note: Only owners of the colony can use this command.
options.name: Can Players Use the Home TP Command

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc colony info command?
options.name: Can Players Use the Show Colony Info Command

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc citizens kill command?
options.name: Can Players Use the Kill Citizens Command

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc colony addOfficer command?
options.name: Can Players Use the Add Officer Command

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc colony delete command?
options.name: Can Players Use the Delete Colony Command

options.description: Should players be allowed to use the /mc colony requestsystem-reset command?
options.name: Can Players Use the Request System Reset Command

categories.description: All configurations related to the research system
categories.name: Research Settings

options.description: Allows automatic and (near) instant completion of research for players in creative mode. If false, creative players will still be able to begin researches, but will have normal progress rates.
options.name: Research Creative Completion

options.description: Significantly increases the amount of information related to research datapacks that is logged during the world load.
options.name: Log Research Data

options.description: A list of items to charge players when undoing an already-completed research.
options.name: Cost to Undo a Completed Research

categories.description: All configuration items related to the core gameplay
categories.name: Gameplay Settings

options.description: Amount of initial citizens.
options.name: Initital Citizen Count

options.description: Should players be able to place an infinite amount of supply camps/ships?
options.name: Infinite Supply Camps/Ships

options.description: Should players be allowed to abandon their colony to create a new one easily? Note: This is buggy!
options.name: Infinite Colonies

options.description: Should colonies in other dimensions be allowed?
options.name: Other Dim Colonies

options.description: Max citizens in one colony.
options.name: Citizen Cap

options.description: Should development features be enabled (might be buggy)?
options.name: Dev Features

options.description: Should citizen name tags be rendered?
options.name: Name Tags

options.description: Should workers work during the rain?
options.name: Working in Rain

options.description: Chance for the Miner to get an ore when mining cobblestone or stone (by default, can be expanded with datapacks to other materials). This is a percentage. (To change which ores the Miner can find, see "List of Lucky Ores".)
options.name: Ore Chance

options.description: The minimum level a Town Hall has to be to allow teleportation to allied colonies.
options.name: Town Hall Teleport

options.description: Food consumption modifier.
options.name: Food Modifier

options.description: How common diseases are. 1 = Very common, 100 = extremely rare.
options.name: Disease Modifier

options.description: If part of the colony is loaded by an owner/officer, should the colony be kept loaded? (Set how many chunks are loaded with the "Colony Chunk Loading Strictness" option.)
options.name: Chunk Load Colony

options.description: Set how long chunks stay loaded after player leaves, does not persist through restarts. Default: 10min
options.name: Colony chunk loading duration

options.description: This controls how many chunks are loaded with the "Chunk Load Colony" option. The higher this value, the fewer chunks will be loaded. (The innermost chunks will be loaded first.) 1 = load all claimed chunks.
options.name: Colony Chunk Loading Strictness

options.description: Max log count in one tree for the Forester to check during their tree search.
options.name: Max Tree Size

options.description: Disables supply camp placing restrictions, intended for skyworlds and similar
options.name: No supply camp placing restrictions

options.description: Raiders will spawn in the sky if this is enabled
options.name: Enable raiders to spawn in the sky
